title: Storing Characters in the DBMS
text: Storing Characters in the DBMS Characters in meta character set Font 0 are sent to the DBMS as one-byte seven-bit characters in the range 32 through 126. Characters in Font 1 are sent as one-byte eight-bit characters in the range 160 through 254. Each character mapped to Fonts 2 through 7 is sent to the database as a three-byte unit. The first byte is a nonprintable character (decimal value 25) which indicates ‘shift font’. The second byte is a decimal value in the range 32 through 94 indicating into which font and shape to switch. The last byte is the seven- or eight-bit character. Figure 1. Example of Font and Shape Information Normal character in the ‘V’ position of Font 6, that is, ‘6.V’: When stored in the DBMS, font number and shape definitions are combined in a single number. This is shown in the table. For example, the number for a bold character in Font 4 is 36. Table 1. Font and Shape Numbers Shape Font 0 1* 2 3* 4 5* 6 7* Normal 0 0 2 2 4 4 6 6 Underline 8 8 10 10 12 12 14 14 Italics 16 16 18 18 20 20 22 22 Italics and underline 24 24 26 26 28 28 30 30 Bold 32 32 34 34 36 36 38 38 Bold and underline 40 40 42 42 44 44 46 46 Bold and italics 48 48 50 50 52 52 54 54 Bold, italics, and underline 56 56 58 58 60 60 62 63 Characters in the fonts marked with an asterisk are always stored as eight-bit characters, that is, in the decimal range 160 through 255. If Uniface encounters an even number in the second byte, it checks to see whether the character in the third byte is seven or eight bits to determine in which of the two possible font and shape numbers the character belongs. Characters below decimal value 32 are only available in Font 0. However, ten of these are reserved by Uniface. Note: The characters in the nonprinting ASCII range (decimal 0 through 31) are interpreted by Uniface and are not available for use in user data. Related Links Meta Character Sets Meta character sets are used for translating installable character sets and defining translation tables. In earlier versions of Uniface, meta character sets were used as internal character sets instead of Unicode. Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
